examples.text:
كَمْ أُعَانِي مِمَّا كَتَبْتُ عَذَابًا
وَيُعَانِي فِي شَرْقِنَا الشُّرَفَاءُ
Kam ʾuʿānī mi'm-mā katabtu ʿaḏāba'ʷ
examples.english: How much do I suffer as a punishment from what I have written? / And [oh, how] our honoured ones in the East are suffering!

etymology.text: From the verb شَرُفَ (šarufa, “to be noble”).
